title: Complexity, Timing, Profiling, Memory, and Capacity · Behavior Case: Performance Hypothesis
summary: A team optimizes string concatenation from one warm run, reports elapsed time without input size, never measures memory, and ships a faster tokenizer that changes word policy. Predict the user decision, input identity, text policy, expected report, failure boundary, and evidence that could disprove the proposed implementation before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the performance hypothesis increment in correctness-preserving capacity study. Learners progress thro…
objectives: State workload, input scale, expected complexity, environment, metric, budget, and correctness invariant before measuring.
Prove tests fail under a deliberate changed behavior and protect against order, locale, encoding, platform, and stale-fixture coupling.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Predict Performance Hypothesis · Theory Performance Hypothesis
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Bookbot team handles case 5243 during an offline public-library reading report. Build deterministic Python evidence to measure representative time and memory while preserving byte-for-byte correctness; test empty, malformed, Unicode, tied, large, changed-policy, and rejected cases before accepting output; then introduce an equal-frequency tie and prove the secondary ranking key is stable. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example Bookbot team handles case 5243 during an offline public-library reading report. Build deterministic Python evidence to measure representative time and memory while preserving byte-for-byte correctness; test empty, malformed, Unicode, tied, large, changed-policy, and rejected cases before accepting output; then introduce an equal-frequency tie and prove the secondary ranking key is stable. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. A defensible solution applies Performance Hypothesis, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to correctness-preserving capacity study.

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-worked-bookbot-performance-profiling-5
# Competency: bookbot-worked-bookbot-performance-profiling-5.
# Case 9036: a digital-humanities corpus revision.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed boundary, causal evidence, repair, regression test, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: install the built wheel in a fresh environment and rerun success, invalid-argument, and decode-failure cases.
def worked_bookbot_performance_profiling_5(tokens: int = 10000, elapsed_ms: float = 42.0, peak_kib: int = 640, time_budget_ms: float = 100.0, memory_budget_kib: int = 1024, output_equal: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "corpusworked5",
        "policy5dekrow",
        "revision9036",
        "commandworked59036",
        "report5dekrow9036",
        "test9036worked5",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if tokens < 1 or elapsed_ms < 0.0 or peak_kib < 0:
        return False, elapsed_ms, "measurement-shape"
    if elapsed_ms > time_budget_ms:
        return False, elapsed_ms, "time-budget"
    if peak_kib > memory_budget_kib:
        return False, peak_kib, "memory-budget"
    if not output_equal:
        return False, elapsed_ms, "semantic-regression"
    assert elapsed_ms <= time_budget_ms
    return True, round(elapsed_ms, 3), "profiled-capacity"

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-performance-hypothesis-qcv6di
# Competency: bookbot-performance-hypothesis.
# Case 7574: a large-file migration rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: test empty, malformed, Unicode, tied, large, changed-policy, and rejected cases before accepting output.

def evidence_bookbot_performance_hypothesis_qcv6di(tokens: int = 10000, elapsed_ms: float = 42.0, peak_kib: int = 640, time_budget_ms: float = 100.0, memory_budget_kib: int = 1024, output_equal: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:

    evidence_axes_qcv6di = (
        "corpusqcv6di",
        "policyid6vcq",
        "revision7574",
        "commandqcv6di7574",
        "reportid6vcq7574",
        "test7574qcv6di",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_qcv6di)) == 6
    if tokens < 1 or elapsed_ms < 0.0 or peak_kib < 0:
        return False, elapsed_ms, "measurement-shape"
    if elapsed_ms > time_budget_ms:
        return False, elapsed_ms, "time-budget"
    if peak_kib > memory_budget_kib:
        return False, peak_kib, "memory-budget"
    if not output_equal:
        return False, elapsed_ms, "semantic-regression"
    assert elapsed_ms <= time_budget_ms
    return True, round(elapsed_ms, 3), "profiled-capacity"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: State workload, input scale, expected complexity, environment, metric, budget, and correctness invariant before measuring.

hints: For Bookbot and Python Defend the Performance Hypothesis model · Theory Performance Hypothesis, name the stakeholder, corpus authority, revision, path, bytes, encoding, Unicode, token, count, rank, report, command, test, package, release, and transfer boundary, Performance Hypothesis precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Bookbot and Python Defend the Performance Hypothesis model · Theory Performance Hypothesis, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, stdout, stderr, status, test, package, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory Bookbot team handles case 8961 during a clean-environment package release. Build deterministic Python evidence to measure representative time and memory while preserving byte-for-byte correctness; preserve original text and offsets beside every normalized comparison key and report exclusion; then install the built wheel in a fresh environment and rerun success, invalid-argument, and decode-failure cases. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: State workload, input scale, expected complexity, environment, metric, budget, and correctness invariant before measuring.

For Bookbot and Python Defend the Performance Hypothesis model · Theory Performance Hypothesis, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Any microbenchmark result is meaningful without a workload model.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: bookbot-performance-hypothesis-qcv6di.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: bookbot-performance-hypothesis-qcv6di(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_bookbot_performance_hypothesis_qcv6di\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?tokens < 1)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?elapsed_ms > time_budget_ms)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?peak_kib > memory_budget_kib)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not output_equal)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
checks.failureMessage: Complete the full function, prompt contract, or gate record shown in the brief.
